item.title: Sheikh Mujib’s birth anniv today
item.description: Today is the 106th birth anniversary of Bangladesh’s founding president Sheikh Mujibur Rahman....

item.title: Iran defiant, hits across region
item.description: Iran said it was ready to take the Middle East war ‘as far as necessary’ as it launched strikes across the region on Monday, while Donald Trump piled pressure on world powers to help reopen a vital shipping lane....

item.title: Fuel oil crisis persists across country
item.description: Consumers across the country are still suffering from shortage of fuel oils at filling stations one day after the government lifted the rationing....

item.title: JU student killing triggers protests on campus
item.description: The killing of Jahangirnagar University student Sharmin Jahan triggered protests while a Dhaka district court sent her husband to jail on Monday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: BCB raises concern over probe committee
item.description: Bangladesh Cricket Board on Monday raised its concern over the investigation committee formed by the National Cricket Council to probe the last board of directors’ election. ...

item.title: Stop attacks on Iran, civic group urges US, Israel
item.description: A civil society organisation on Monday urged the United States and Israel to stop their joint military attacks on Iran immediately....

item.title: Several suspects arrested for Shaheed Minar murder
item.description: The police have detained several suspects in connection with the murder of social media content creator Rakib Ahmed, who was shot dead by unidentified miscreants in the capital’s Central Shaheed Minar area on Sunday night....

item.title: Ganatantrik Jukta Front for resignation of foreign minister
item.description: Leaders of Ganatantrik Jukta Front on Monday at a protest rally in Dhaka demanded immediate resignation of foreign minister Khalilur Rahman for signing unequal trade deals with the United States government....News website membership

item.title: Trade deficit widens as import bill climbs
item.description: Bangladesh’s trade deficit widened in the first seven months of the current fiscal year as imports increased while export earnings declined, adding pressure on the country’s external balance......

item.title: BB governor presses for quick 5-bank merger
item.description: Bangladesh Bank governor Mostaqur Rahman on Monday instructed officials to accelerate the merger process of five weak Shariah-based banks into Sammilito Islami Bank, including the integration of their information technology systems......

item.title: 180-day action plan unveiled to transform investment climate
item.description: The Bangladesh Investment Development Authority unveiled a comprehensive 180-day joint action plan on Sunday evening to overhaul the country’s investment climate......

item.title: NBR launches online system for return submission deadline extension
item.description: The National Board of Revenue has introduced an online system allowing individual taxpayers to apply for an extension of time to submit their income tax returns for the 2025-2026 tax year......Maps

item.title: Weaving craft under spotlight at handloom exhibition
item.description: A colourful exhibition and fair celebrating Bangladesh’s rich handloom heritage concluded on Monday in the capital, drawing large crowds of visitors eager to explore the country’s traditional weaving industry and handcrafted textiles......

item.title: Anthropogenic threats to bonding life to life
item.description: THE history of life on Earth is inseparable from the history of connection. From the first primitive cells to the sprawling ecosystems of today, living organisms have thrived not in isolation but through cooperation, interdependence and mutual support. ‘Bonding life to life’ is not mere metaphor; it is the fundamental principle on which life itself depends. No organism exists......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Financial literacy as everyday power
item.description: IN BANGLADESH, conversations about economic development tend to revolve around export figures, remittance inflows and headline growth rates. Yet beneath these macroeconomic indicators lies a quieter, more intimate question: how well are individuals equipped to manage their own money? Financial literacy, often treated as a technical or secondary concern, is in......

item.title: Public health obligations as rights
item.description: PUBLIC health safety has become a major issues in Bangladesh, which is faces major challenges in ensuring quality healthcare services in public hospitals. The number of physicians available for health services also remains disproportionately low compared with the needs......

item.title: Strengthening urban primary health care
item.description: THE primary healthcare system of urban Bangladesh is beset with a multitude of complexities due to rapid urbanisation and a lack of adequate infrastructure, particularly in primary healthcare. According to the World Health Organisation, rapid urbanisation is the most significant global trend in the 21st century, exerting substantial impact on the healthcare system of......News website membership

item.title: Workers must be paid before Eid
item.description: WITH less than a week to Eid-ul-Fitr, many industrial units have still not paid workers wages or the festival allowance. The Industrial Police reported on March 15 that 3,626 factories, 32.34 per cent of all industrial units, had not paid February wages and 6,408 units, or 63.45 per cent, had still withheld the allowance for Eid. A labour ministry report, based on findings of the law......

item.title: Govt should stop making populist announcement
item.description: DESPITE the withdrawal of fuel oil rationing on March 15, vehicle owners and farmers are reportedly still facing shortage. Long queues of cars and motorcycles at filling stations show that supply has yet to stabilise. The situation suggests that the government has lifted the rationing imposed on March 6 without first ensuring the supply chain. The announcement, therefore......Bangladesh news updates
